note: Robert H. Berk, 'Limiting Behavior of Posterior Distributions when the Model is Incorrect', The Annals of Mathematical Statistics 37 (1966) 51-58 (received 23 Apr 1965). Read via page images (8p scan; pdftotext returned only JSTOR boilerplate). This IS the classical Bayesian-under-misspecification concentration result. Berk defines eta(theta)=E_F H(X|theta)=E_F log u(X|theta) with u(x|theta)=g(x)f(x|theta) (Eq.2.1), eta*=sup_theta eta, and the asymptotic carrier A0={theta: eta(theta)=eta*} (Eq.2.3). Eq.(2.2, p.53): eta(theta) <= E log f(X) with equality PRECISELY when f(x|theta) is also a density for F; Berk states eta 'measures the similarity between F and f(.|theta)', is 'essentially a measure of entropy', and cites Kullback [6]. Hence maximizing eta == minimizing KL(F||f_theta), so A0 is exactly the set of KL projections/KL-minimizers of the model onto the true F. Main Theorem (Sec 4, p.54): under Assumptions (i)-(iv), if X_i are e.i.d. ~ F (F need NOT be any model density), the posterior P_k is a.s.[F] asymptotically carried on A0 -- for every open U containing A0, lim_k P_k(U)=1 [F]. Sec 5 confirms A0 is in general distinct from any true parameter (correct-model case A0={theta_0} is a special case; misspecified normal model gives A0={EX} or {(EX,Cov X)}), and A0 may contain more than one point. Both manuscript claims (C127, C128) invoke exactly this classical result and apply it correctly to the coupled/misspecified-update-likelihood setting; the reweighted update likelihood u(x|theta)=g(x)f(x|theta) that Berk works with (Le Cam's device) is precisely a 'coupled update likelihood', making C128's use particularly on-point.

C127.evidence: Direct support (LAYER 2). L408 App-Formal claim: 'the posterior concentrates on the Kullback-Leibler projection theta-dagger [berk1966], in general distinct from theta-star.' Berk's Main Theorem (Sec 4, p.54): 'If {X_i} are actually e.i.d. with distribution F, and Assumptions (i)-(iv) hold, then P_k is almost surely [F] asymptotically carried on a set A0 (see Equation (2.3)) in the sense that if U is an open set containing A0, lim P_k U = 1 [F].' The carrier is A0={theta: eta(theta)=eta*} (Eq.2.3, p.53), the MAXIMIZERS of eta(theta)=E_F log f(X|theta). Eq.(2.2, p.53): 'eta(theta) <= E log f(X); there is equality in (2.2) precisely when f(x|theta) is also a density for F. In a sense then eta(theta) measures the similarity between F and f(.|theta)' -- Berk labels eta 'essentially a measure of entropy; see [6]' (Kullback, Information Theory and Statistics). Maximizing E_F log f(X|theta) is identically minimizing KL(F||f_theta) (the E_F log f_F term is theta-free), so A0 = the KL-projection set = theta-dagger. 'Distinct from theta-star' is directly supported by Sec 5 (p.56-57): the correct-model case where f(.|theta_0) is a density for F gives A0={theta_0} only as a SPECIAL case; when the model is incorrect A0 is a different point/set (e.g. a misspecified normal model concentrates on the true mean/covariance EX, (EX,Cov X), NOT on any model 'true value'). The manuscript's 'theta-dependent (non-affine) coupled validator' inducing misspecification is a valid instance of Berk's F-not-in-model setup. Citation is accurate and correctly attributed.

C128.evidence: Direct support (LAYER 2). L411 App-Formal claim: 'A coupled, misspecified update likelihood places the posterior at its KL minimizer [berk1966].' Berk explicitly works with a REWEIGHTED/coupled update likelihood: Sec 2, Eq.(2.1, p.52): 'This equation [posterior 1.1] remains valid if f(.|theta) is replaced by a function of the form u(x|theta)=g(x)f(x|theta)', following Le Cam's device u=f(.|theta)/f(.|theta_0). Setting H(.|theta)=log u(.|theta), the entropy functional is eta(theta)=E_F H(X|theta) and the asymptotic carrier A0={theta: eta(theta)=eta*} is its argmax (Eq.2.3). Main Theorem (Sec 4, p.54) then places the posterior on A0: lim P_k U = 1 [F] for open U superset A0. Since A0 maximizes E_F log (model-likelihood), it is the KL-minimizer of the (coupled, possibly misspecified) update likelihood toward the true F (Eq.2.2 equality iff the likelihood is a density for F). Thus 'a coupled, misspecified update likelihood places the posterior at its KL minimizer' is exactly Berk's Main Theorem specialized to a single (unique) minimizer. Accurate and well-attributed; the coupled/reweighted-likelihood framing matches Berk's u(x|theta)=g(x)f(x|theta) construction unusually closely.

reverse_findings: LAYER 3 boundary (applies to BOTH C127 'concentrates on theta-dagger' and C128 'places the posterior AT its KL minimizer'): Berk proves topological CONCENTRATION on the carrier A0, NOT convergence to a limiting posterior distribution over A0. His Sec 5 counterexample (Theta={0,1} with eta(0)=eta(1), so A0={0,1}) shows L_k=P_k(1)/P_k(0)=(p/q)prod r(X_i) with log L_k an e.i.d. sum, giving limsup L_k=+inf, liminf L_k=0 [F], hence limsup P_k(1)=1, liminf P_k(1)=0 -- the posterior OSCILLATES with no stability on A0. Therefore the singular 'the KL projection theta-dagger' / 'AT its KL minimizer' phrasing is exact only when the KL-argmin is UNIQUE (A0 a singleton -> Sec 5: posterior a.s. degenerate there). If the manuscript's coupled-validator misspecification can produce a multi-point KL-argmin set, the point-mass language is an idealization, not a theorem. Not a defect for C127/C128 as written (both presuppose a unique theta-dagger), but the manuscript should ensure theta-dagger is assumed/argued unique wherever it leans on convergence rather than mere concentration.

LAYER 3 scope: Berk assumes i.i.d.-type (e.i.d.) observations under a single FIXED true distribution F, plus regularity Assumptions (i)-(iv) (joint measurability + a.e. continuity of f(x|theta); F{f(x|theta)>0}=1; local integrability E||H||_inf<inf; a U-statistic tail/boundedness condition relaxing Wald 1949). If the manuscript's 'coupled, theta-dependent (non-affine) validator' induces adaptive/dependent or theta-dependent data generation (i.e. the sampling distribution itself changes with theta or across rounds), Berk 1966 is the correct classical ANCHOR for the static/i.i.d. misspecified-projection idealization but does not by itself cover non-i.i.d./adaptive coupling. Fine as a foundational cite; flagged only so the appendix does not silently transfer the i.i.d. theorem to a genuinely sequential/dependent regime without comment.

LAYER 3 identity check (not a defect): Berk's carrier maximizes eta(theta)=E_F log u(X|theta) for the possibly-reweighted u(x|theta)=g(x)f(x|theta). The clean identification 'argmax eta == argmin KL(F||f_theta)' holds when the reweighting g(x) does not move the argmax (Eq.2.2 uses u a proper density-comparison), i.e. when the coupled update likelihood is normalized/proper. The manuscript's 'KL minimizer' is the right object provided theta-dagger is defined as argmin_theta KL(true || model-under-coupled-likelihood); worth a one-line confirmation that the appendix's theta-dagger is that same KL projection Berk's eta-argmax gives.

Bib-metadata note (not inspected against references.bib in this task): canonical citation is Robert H. Berk, 'Limiting Behavior of Posterior Distributions when the Model is Incorrect', Ann. Math. Statist. 37(1) (1966) 51-58. Confirm year/volume/pages and single-author 'Berk, Robert H.' in the .bib.
